title: leadership
subtitle: community building, tech events & academic initiatives
intro: Beyond code, I've helped build and sustain tech communities through event organization, partnerships, and student leadership across multiple semesters.

events.name: Software Tech 2025
events.description: Flagship multi-segment technology event combining technical challenges, workshops, and interdisciplinary collaboration.
events.purpose: To bring together students, industry professionals, and educators to explore emerging technologies and foster hands-on learning through competitions and workshops.
myContributions: Led public relations and external communications
Designed event branding and promotional materials
Hosted opening ceremony and facilitated panel transitions
highlights: Workshops on Web Development and Web3

events.name: UX/UI Workshop Series
events.description: Three-day workshop series focused on UX/UI, data, and business strategy.
events.purpose: To provide students with practical UX/UI skills and connect design thinking with data analytics and business strategy.
myContributions: Delivered Day 1 presentation on UX fundamentals
Managed AV setup and live demonstrations
highlights: Day 1: UX fundamentals (Cynthia, Patrick, Mr. Félix)
Day 2: UX + Data with Yessica Flores
Day 3: UX + Business with Roberto Bonilla

events.name: Cybersecurity Awareness Month
events.description: Awareness talk and collaboration with ESET as part of Cybersecurity Month.
events.purpose: To raise awareness about cybersecurity best practices and establish lasting industry partnerships.
myContributions: Initiated and negotiated partnership with ESET
Coordinated logistics for industry speaker visit

events.name: Women in STEM Forum (8M)
events.description: Panel highlighting the experiences of women students in STEM fields.
events.purpose: To celebrate and amplify the voices of women in STEM, providing role models and fostering discussion on representation.
myContributions: Curated and invited panel of 4 outstanding female students
Developed discussion questions and panel structure
highlights: Focused on representation and career growth
Part of International Women's Day activities

events.name: AI & Society Forum
events.description: Interdisciplinary forum on Artificial Intelligence in collaboration with the Policy Club.
events.purpose: To explore the societal implications of AI through cross-disciplinary dialogue between tech and policy students.
myContributions: Co-designed forum format with Policy Club leadership
highlights: Discussion on AI impact and ethics
